quick_facts.label: Employer and status
quick_facts.value: Postal Assistant/Sorting Assistant is a Department of Posts, Ministry of Communications role classified in the recruitment rules as General Central Service, Group ‘C’, non-gazetted and ministerial.

quick_facts.label: Current SSC examination context
quick_facts.value: The SSC Combined Graduate Level Examination 2025 notice lists Postal Assistant/Sorting Assistant in the Department of Posts as a Pay Level-4 Group ‘C’ post with an advertised age band of 18–27 years. Recruitment routes and eligibility must be checked against the particular SSC/Department notice because they can change by cycle.

quick_facts.label: Education and computer requirement
quick_facts.value: The 2022 Department of Posts recruitment rules prescribe a bachelor’s degree from a recognised university or institute and knowledge of working on a computer for direct recruits; the rules contain a separate 10+2 provision for specified serving Gramin Dak Sevaks.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: Pay is variable under government pay rules and allowances. The Department’s 2022 rules and SSC’s 2025 notice identify PA/SA at Pay Matrix Level 4, ₹25,500–81,100; this is the pay-matrix range, not a guaranteed in-hand salary.

quick_facts.label: Posting liability
quick_facts.value: The recruitment rules state that an appointee may be required to serve anywhere in India, including in Army Postal Services.

sections.heading: What Postal Assistants and Sorting Assistants do
paragraphs: PA/SA is an operational clerical role, but the work differs by posting. India Post’s organisational material describes Postal Assistant work across mails, sorting, delivery, dispatch, registration, Speed Post, parcels, money orders, sub-accounts/cash or treasury, accounts, and post-office savings products and certificates. In practice, that means handling prescribed transactions and records, using departmental systems, and serving customers where assigned.

Sorting Assistant work in Railway Mail Service is specifically mail-processing work. The Postal Manual makes the Sorting Assistant responsible for sorting unregistered articles received at the office, preparing assigned mail bags for dispatch, ensuring articles are correctly sorted and handed over on time, and preparing prescribed bundles. The manual emphasizes avoiding missent mail, so accuracy and adherence to sorting instructions are core responsibilities.
bullets: Postal Assistant assignments can combine counter-facing, transaction-processing, mail/parcel, registration, dispatch, savings and records work.
Sorting Assistant assignments centre on correct, timely sorting and preparation of mail for onward dispatch.
The exact mix depends on the sanctioned PA/SA stream and the office or Railway Mail Service unit to which the employee is allocated.

sections.heading: Entry routes and eligibility
paragraphs: The node’s historical parent is SSC CHSL, but applicants should not assume that CHSL is the active route. The official SSC Combined Graduate Level Examination 2025 notice lists PA/SA as a Department of Posts Group ‘C’ Level-4 post. The Department’s recruitment rules also expressly contemplate open competitive recruitment under a Department-approved scheme, alongside internal and Gramin Dak Sevak routes. Therefore, use the live SSC notice for the relevant examination year—not an older coaching-site pattern—to determine the actual application route, vacancy, age cut-off, examination stages, preferences and document requirements.

For the direct-recruit category in the 2022 rules, the prescribed baseline is a recognised bachelor’s degree plus working knowledge of computers. The rules give 18–27 as the direct-recruit age band, subject to applicable relaxations and the SSC-advertised crucial date where recruitment is through SSC. A bachelor’s degree such as BA, B.Com or BCA can meet the degree-level requirement if awarded by a recognised institution; the rules do not prescribe one named discipline.
bullets: Track official SSC notices and vacancy/preference updates for the examination cycle in which PA/SA is offered.
Confirm degree recognition, age cut-off, category-based relaxations, computer/skill-test conditions and post preference rules in that cycle’s notice.
Serving eligible departmental staff and specified Gramin Dak Sevaks may have separate competitive or promotion-based avenues under the Department’s rules.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: The rules expressly require working knowledge of computers, while the role’s prescribed activities demand reliable data and transaction handling. For Sorting Assistant work, the Postal Manual’s focus on correct sorting, dispatch timing and prevention of missent articles makes sustained attention to detail and procedural discipline especially important. For Postal Assistant work, clear customer communication, numerical and record accuracy, and the ability to learn product and process rules are practical complements to the formal eligibility requirement.
bullets: Working knowledge of computers and keyboard-based data entry.
Accuracy with addresses, PIN-code/sorting instructions, records and cash or transaction procedures.
Customer communication and service orientation for counter or public-facing assignments.
Ability to follow written procedures, safeguard entrusted items and meet dispatch timelines.

sections.heading: Workplace and service conditions
paragraphs: PA/SA roles are within the Department of Posts rather than a single uniform desk job. The 2022 rules cover Postal Assistant roles in post offices and administrative/circle settings, and Sorting Assistant roles in Railway Mail Service; a PA role may therefore be customer-facing or back-office, while an SA role is normally tied to mail sorting and dispatch operations. The rules also make appointees liable for service anywhere in India, including Army Postal Services.

Direct recruits have a two-year probation period under the relevant rules, subject to successful completion of the specified induction training delivered by a Postal Training Centre. The training title varies with the PA/SA stream, so candidates should expect role-specific induction rather than treating all PA/SA postings as identical.
bullets: Possible settings include post offices, circle/regional or other administrative offices, and Railway Mail Service operations.
Assignments may change the balance among public service, data/records work, transaction handling and mail-processing duties.
National mobility is a stated service condition.

sections.heading: Departmental progression context
paragraphs: The Department’s PA/SA recruitment rules provide internal advancement routes into PA/SA through Limited Departmental Competitive Examinations for eligible officials in specified lower Pay Matrix levels, while other vacancies are filled through open competition or a competitive examination limited to eligible Gramin Dak Sevaks. After appointment, advancement is governed by the applicable service rules, qualifying service and departmental examinations rather than by an automatic academic-degree pathway.

The 2024 amendment to the PA/SA rules specifically addresses how senior officials may be considered when juniors become eligible for Limited Departmental Competitive Examination or Competitive Examination for promotion to the next higher grade. This confirms that departmental examinations are a meaningful progression mechanism, but exact eligibility, vacancies, syllabus and next-post outcomes should be verified in the relevant current departmental notification.
bullets: Departmental examinations can matter both for entry from eligible feeder cadres and for later promotion.
Qualifying service, probation completion and the applicable feeder cadre influence eligibility.
A distance-learning or regular bachelor’s degree may be personally useful, but promotion is controlled by the notified service and examination rules.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As of the cited 2025 SSC notice and the Department’s PA/SA recruitment rules, the post is shown at Pay Matrix Level 4: ₹25,500–81,100. That range is a dated pay-level reference, not a take-home-pay figure. Actual monthly pay varies with the applicable central-government pay rules, location-linked allowances, deductions, posting, and later revisions; candidates should rely on the appointment terms and current government orders for a personal estimate.
bullets: Do not treat the top of the matrix or an online ‘in-hand salary’ estimate as starting pay.
Allowances and deductions are variable and should be checked at the time of appointment.
